intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text: Good! I see there are actually **three** samples here (GSM5746268, GSM5746269, GSM5746270), each with a 10x triplet. You asked for the one prefixed GSM5746268, so I'll focus on that. Let me read the Seurat workflow recipe first, then present a plan.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Now let me present the workflow plan:

input.title: Seurat scRNA-seq workflow on GSM5746268
input.summary: Load 10x triplet, QC, normalize, cluster, and identify top markers per cluster.

steps.title: Load 10x matrix triplet
steps.description: Read .mtx, .features, .barcodes files for GSM5746268 and create a Seurat object with standard filtering (min.cells=3, min.features=200).

steps.title: QC: compute mitochondrial fraction & filter
steps.description: Calculate percent.mt, visualize nFeature_RNA and percent.mt distributions, then subset to reasonable thresholds (we'll inspect first and use sensible defaults).

steps.title: Normalize, find HVG, scale, PCA
steps.description: NormalizeData, FindVariableFeatures, ScaleData, RunPCA with 30 dims as default (adjustable if elbow plot suggests otherwise).

steps.title: Neighbors, clustering, UMAP
steps.description: FindNeighbors, FindClusters (resolution 0.5 default), RunUMAP, then JoinLayers before marker detection.

steps.title: Find all markers & visualize
steps.description: FindAllMarkers with standard thresholds (logfc.threshold=0.25, min.pct=0.1), then create a UMAP plot and a table of top markers per cluster.

assumptions: Loading GSM5746268 sample only (the first triplet in DATA_DIR)
Using standard Seurat QC defaults: min.cells=3, min.features=200
Using default mitochondrial gene prefix pattern (^MT- for mouse/human; will inspect features to confirm)
Using 30 PCs for PCA, resolution 0.5 for clustering, default UMAP params — will show diagnostics and can adjust if needed
R/Seurat v5 with modern layer idioms
